steps.title: Check current prometheus deployment
steps.description:
Verify your prometheus version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
helm list -n prometheus 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working prometheus installation.

steps.title: Review prometheus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus
```
## Proposal
**Use case. Why is this important?**
[MAD using median](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Median_absolute_deviation)
[MAD using mean](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Average_absolute_deviation)
Mean/Median absolute deviation around a central

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add `mad_over_time` function
steps.description:
About adding `mad(...)` etc.:

The problem is that we already tried once to happily add a fairly advanced statistical function ([`holt_winters()`](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/querying/functions/#holt_winters)). The result was that many users were confused what it actually means
```yaml
quantile_over_time(0.5,
    (
        abs(
            value - quantile_over_time(0.5, value[T:])
        )
    )[T:]
)
```

steps.title: Upgrade prometheus to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade prometheus:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade prometheus prometheus/prometheus --namespace prometheus
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus
helm list -n prometheus
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add `mad_over_time` function" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
About adding `mad(...)` etc.:

The problem is that we already tried once to happily add a fairly advanced statistical function ([`holt_winters()`](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/querying/functions/#holt_winters)).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
